'''Bancar''' was a celestial body<ref name="Abduction">''[[The Abduction]]''</ref> located in the [[Bancar system/Legends|Bancar system]], a part of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Rayter sector/Legends|Rayter sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Around the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], [[Nak Farool]], [[Special Assistant]] to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Nile Owen]] of the Rayter [[Sector/Legends|sector]], dispatched an Imperial [[II-xC Maintenance/Broadcast Ship]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Narg]] on a [[Operation 45RA|mission]] to the [[Laim system/Legends|Laim system]]. The [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] crew stored a flight plan for a return trip to Narg into the [[Computer/Legends|computer]] system of the vessel, and while the ship was in the Laim [[Star system/Legends|system]], a flight plan for further transit to Bancar was also being calculated. However, at that time, the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[VR-10 Cuirilla-Rayl Xylines-class spacetug|spacetug]] ''[[Worthless Fool]]'', the crew of which had learned of the Imperial activities in the Laim system, arrived there and captured the Imperial broadcast ship.<ref name="Abduction" />

==Behind the scenes==
Bancar was introduced in ''[[The Abduction]]'', a [[1992]] [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] adventure authored by [[Chuck Truett]] and published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Abduction" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Bancar system, and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square J-19.<ref name="TEA" />

==Appearances==
